summary: Iteration 9 regression retest of /api/portfolio data-isolation fix. Ran full test_auth.py suite (36 tests). All 36 passed including the four targeted isolation tests: test_portfolio_lots_isolated, test_non_admin_admin_all_silently_ignored_on_portfolio, test_admin_portfolio_without_admin_all_is_scoped, test_admin_portfolio_admin_all_cross_user.
critical_code_review_comments: get_portfolio scope merge using dict(scope) with conditional existence-guard is correct - prevents overwriting per-user pinning while still excluding null/missing user_id docs for admin_all path.
seed_data_creation: None - tests use isolated registered users via fixtures
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 8 regression (portfolio isolation overwrite) is fully resolved in iter 9. test_auth.py is now a stable 36/36 pass baseline. Other test files (test_portfolio.py, test_trades.py, test_alerts.py, backend_test.py) were not executed in this iteration since the request was a targeted regression check.
rca of the issue: Iter 8 introduced a bug where the admin_all existence-guard ({'user_id': {'$exists': True}}) was unconditionally added to the match pipeline, overwriting per-user scope pinning. Iter 9 fix uses dict(scope) and only adds the existence-guard when 'user_id' is not already pinned, preserving per-user scoping for non-admin and admin-without-admin_all paths. Regression verified passing.
